name: Router Speaker
description: The `router` speaker platform allows you to switch which [speaker component](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/) receives audio at runtime. The router acts as a single speaker that an audio producer (such as a [media player](https://esphome.io/components/media_player/) or a [mixer speaker](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/mixer/)) sends audio to, and it forwards that audio to whichever output speaker is currently active. Use the [switch output action](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/router#router_speaker-switch_output) to change the active output while audio is playing, for example to move playback between an I²S speaker and an [SPDIF](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/i2s_audio/#spdif-mode) output.

config_entries.label: Bits Per Sample
config_entries.description: The bit depth of the audio stream. Must be between `8` and `32`. Every output speaker must use this bit depth.

config_entries.label: Num Channels
config_entries.description: The number of audio channels. Either `1` or `2`. Every output speaker must use this number of channels.

config_entries.label: Output Speakers
config_entries.description: A list of [speakers](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/) the router can output to. Must have at least 2 and at most 8 speakers. The first speaker listed is the active output when the device boots.

config_entries.label: Sample Rate
config_entries.description: The sample rate of the audio stream in Hz. Must be between `8000` and `96000`. Every output speaker must use this sample rate.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: The router speaker to control. Defaults to the only one in your YAML.
